sections.vendors: Tesco has published its full F&F garment and footwear factory list — one of the most transparent disclosures among UK supermarket fashion brands. A 40-strong in-house labour standards team operates across key Asian sourcing countries visiting factories daily, pre-approving all sites with zero tolerance for child labour.
sections.sustainability: Tesco is phasing out hazardous chemicals from F&F clothing, having published its January 2025 chemical management update. Tesco was the first UK supermarket to use recycled polyester in garments and is targeting 100% recycled polyester by 2030. As a founding member of ETI and BCI, F&F's sustainability credentials among supermarket peers are strong.
sections.sourcing: No major sourcing country changes announced. Tesco is pursuing an online-only F&F expansion model following strong digital performance — product strategy is shifting toward tighter trend-led edits with fewer high-volume repeat styles and growing emphasis on longevity and responsible sourcing.
sections.strategy: F&F partnered with the Graduate Fashion Foundation as key partner for the 35th Graduate Fashion Week (15–18 June 2026 at Truman Brewery, London) — a deliberate move to position F&F as a fashion-forward brand, not just a supermarket label. Tesco is also expanding F&F via online-only in new markets.
sections.pricing: Graduate Fashion Week partnership and online-only expansion suggest F&F is willing to test slightly elevated price points in digital channel. Current supermarket stores maintain value-first pricing consistent with George and TU competition.
sections.launches: F&F was headline partner at Graduate Fashion Week 2026 — the most high-profile brand positioning move for F&F in years, aimed at younger fashion-conscious consumers. Online-only F&F expansion into new markets is underway.
sections.gossip: Graduate Fashion Week partnership is the boldest 'fashion credibility' play any UK supermarket brand has made this year — F&F is clearly trying to distance itself from the 'supermarket basics' perception ahead of a potential standalone brand push.
soWhat: Factory list publication means your factory must be on Tesco's approved list before any direct business — check OSH/Open Supply Hub registration and ensure your factory name appears correctly under F&F records.

100% recycled polyester target by 2030 means F&F buyers will increasingly mandate GRS-certified recycled polyester for synthetic garments — start transitioning polyester jersey and fleece to GRS-cert material now.

Online-only expansion into new markets means Tesco may start sourcing more trend-led, smaller-batch styles — opportunity for factories with agile production and lower MOQs in jersey tops and occasionwear.
